not verified yet · confidence 0.25 · as of 4.6.1

  • Package.swift:7
    View the line on GitHub →
    Package.swift declares no watchOS platform floor (iOS/macOS/tvOS/visionOS only). Missing floor is not absence evidence, so status is unknown.

    View the line on GitHub →
    README platform list (iOS, macOS, tvOS, visionOS, Android, Web) omits watchOS.

not verified yet · confidence 0.20 · as of 4.6.1

  • README.md:0
    View the line on GitHub →
    README platform list omits Linux; 191 of 759 surface decls carry guards resolving absent on linux, and core files import QuartzCore unconditionally, but there is no guard/availability evidence of absence for the core symbols, so status is unknown.

✓ serves it · ◐ with conditions · ✕ not served (proven) · ? not verified yet — hover any verdict for the receipt, click to pin it

Notes

Vector animation playback: Core symbols (LottieAnimationView, LottieAnimationView.play, LottieAnimation.named) are syntactically unguarded, so the surface digest resolves them present even on watchOS/linux; those two platforms are labeled unknown rather than supported because the manifest declares no floor there and the unconditional QuartzCore import leaves compilability unverified.

Badges for your README

Maintainer of lottie-ios? These are yours — verified claims, linked to the receipts. Wrong verdict? That's a fix we want — open an issue on GitHub or see contribute.

Made refreshingly cool from Paris, France
